Tests the unary plus operator with an object that has a custom valueOf method returning -1.

# Purpose
This code defines an object `a` with a custom `valueOf` method that returns `-1`. The `valueOf` method is a built-in JavaScript function that returns the primitive value of an object. In this case, when the unary plus operator (`+`) is applied to the object `a`, it invokes the `valueOf` method, resulting in the expression `+a` evaluating to `-1`. The `assert` statement checks that this evaluation is correct, ensuring that the custom `valueOf` method behaves as expected. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on demonstrating and verifying the behavior of the `valueOf` method in JavaScript.
# Global Variables

---
### a
- **Type**: ``object``
- **Description**: Contains a single method `valueOf` that returns the integer `-1`. This object can be used in operations that require a numeric value, where it will evaluate to `-1`.
- **Use**: Used to provide a custom numeric value when the object is coerced to a number.
